summaryTemplate: {{className}} is the {{patternRole}}. Each handler either processes a request or forwards it to its successor, so the sender is decoupled from which handler ultimately responds. Pattern body lives at {{file}}:{{lineStart}}-{{lineEnd}}.
chosenRationaleTemplate: Tagged as Chain of Responsibility because {{className}} pairs a virtual handle method with a successor link (lines {{lineStart}}-{{lineEnd}}) — the handle-or-forward shape.

chain_handle_method.note: The request handler. It decides whether to process the request or pass it on.

chain_successor_link.note: The successor link. Forwarding to it is what makes this a chain rather than a single handler.

runnerUpComparisonTemplates.State: State swaps the whole object the context delegates to. Chain of Responsibility keeps a linked list of handlers and walks it until one accepts — the successor pointer is the tell.
